question: What's your primary role right now?
description: Pick the one that fits best — you can specify more later.

question: How many years have you been writing code?
description: Count from when you seriously started — school included.

question: What's your age range?
Where are you based?
description: Your current location — not necessarily where you're from.

question: What's your gender?
What's your highest level of education?
description: Doesn't have to be in tech.

options.option_text: University — no degree (dropped out or in progress)

question: How did you actually learn to code?
description: Pick everything that genuinely shaped your skills.

question: What's your primary coding language today?
description: The one you'd reach for first on a new project.

question: Which languages have you used seriously in the past 6 months?
description: Not just touched — actually built with.

question: Which databases are in your current stack?
description: Select all you've used in production or real projects recently.

question: Which cloud platforms or deployment targets do you use?
description: Where your code actually runs.

question: Which web frameworks are part of your daily workflow?
description: Frontend or backend — whatever you're actively building with.

question: What's your IDE or editor of choice?
description: The one open right now (or almost always).

question: What OS do you primarily develop on?
How do you handle state management in your apps?
description: For frontend/fullstack devs.

options.option_text: Built-in framework state (Context, Vue stores, signals)

What's your preferred full-stack setup right now?
Which mobile development approach do you use?
What's your primary design or prototyping tool?
description: For UI/UX designers and frontend devs.

question: What hardware do you use to train or run ML models?
description: For ML/AI engineers and data scientists.

question: What's your main data pipeline or orchestration tool?
description: For data engineers.

question: What cloud deployment strategy do you rely on most?
description: For cloud/DevOps engineers.

question: Which cybersecurity domain takes up most of your time?
description: For security specialists.

question: Which security tool category is most essential to you?
description: For security specialists.

question: Which game engine is your daily driver?
description: For game developers.

question: Which microcontroller or architecture do you work with most?
description: For embedded/hardware engineers.

question: Which blockchain ecosystem are you actively building for?
description: For blockchain developers.

question: How often do you use AI tools in your work?
description: Be honest — passive use counts too.

question: Which AI tools do you actively use in your workflow?
description: Select everything you genuinely use — not just tried once.

question: Where in your workflow does AI actually help you the most?
description: Rank from most to least impactful.

question: How well do AI tools handle the complex tasks you throw at them?
description: 1 = constantly fails me / 5 = handles almost everything

question: Do you think AI is a realistic threat to your current job?
description: No judgment — just your honest read.

options.option_text: Yes — it's already replacing junior roles
Maybe — but my specialization protects me
Unlikely — AI needs humans to guide it
No — AI is just a better tool, not a replacement

question: What worries you most about AI in the tech industry?
description: Pick your top concern.

options.option_text: Concentration of power in a few big companies
Code quality and hallucinations shipped to production
Widening the gap between senior and junior devs
None — I'm mostly optimistic about AI

question: What's your current employment situation?

options.option_text: Running my own business or startup

question: What industry does your main employer or client operate in?
How big is the organization you work with most?
What's your work setup day-to-day?
What are your main sources of freelance work?
description: Pick everything that's brought you paying clients.

options.option_text: Personal referrals and word of mouth
Direct clients from previous work or internships

question: Which payment methods do you actually use to receive income?
description: What works for you in practice.

question: What's your total annual compensation?
description: Salary + bonuses + perks before taxes. DZD if based in Algeria.

question: How do you feel about your current compensation?
description: Relative to your skills and the market.

question: What's the biggest driver behind your compensation level?
description: The thing that most explains where your pay landed.

options.option_text: Specialized skills in a niche area
Company budget — not much room to grow
Market rates in Algeria are just low
I'm still building skills to ask for more

question: How remote-friendly is your current work situation?
description: 1 = fully on-site / 5 = fully remote

question: What's your biggest obstacle to working fully remotely?
description: Pick the one that matters most.

options.option_text: No track record or portfolio for international work
I actually prefer working in person

question: Are you actively looking for remote opportunities with international companies?

options.option_text: Open to it but not actively pursuing
I already work with international clients or companies

question: What would make you choose a remote international job over a local one?
description: Rank your top motivators.

options.option_text: Access to better tech and tooling

question: What are your main professional goals for the next 2 years?
description: Pick up to 3.

options.option_text: Land a remote job with an international company
Build and launch my own product or startup
Move into a leadership or management role
Contribute to open source at a higher level
Publish research or build a public profile

question: How likely are you to still be working in Algeria in 3 years?
description: Be honest.

question: What's the primary reason behind your emigration plans or lack thereof?
description: The real driver — not the polished answer.

options.option_text: No strong reason to leave — life is good here
Career growth I can't find locally
I already left or am in the process
I'm planning to leave but haven't committed yet
The local ecosystem is improving — I'm staying

question: What's your biggest professional challenge right now?
description: The thing that's actually slowing you down.

options.option_text: Lack of mentorship or senior peers

question: What's the biggest obstacle when learning something new in tech?
description: For people actively upskilling.

options.option_text: Too many technologies to choose from
Weak foundations in math or CS theory
No real projects to practice on
Hard to stay consistent with self-study

question: What makes shipping code in Algeria harder than it should be?
description: Infrastructure or ecosystem friction.

options.option_text: No easy access to international payment tools
VPN dependency for basic dev tools
No local cloud or hosting options
Limited local talent to hire or collaborate with
Lack of local investor or VC ecosystem

question: What's your main way of staying up to date with tech?
description: How you actually consume new knowledge.

options.option_text: GitHub trending and open source exploration
Building side projects and learning by doing

question: Rank how you prefer to learn something new from scratch.
description: Most effective to least for you personally.

question: Are you part of any local tech communities or events?
description: Online or in-person.

options.option_text: Yes — I'm active in local communities (Discord, meetups, etc.)
Occasionally — I attend events or follow groups
No — I mostly learn and work solo
I'd like to be but can't find the right community

question: What's holding the Algerian tech ecosystem back the most?
description: Pick your top factor.

options.option_text: Brain brain — the best people leave
Limited access to funding and investment
Cultural friction between traditional institutions and tech
No strong local tech companies to learn from

question: How optimistic are you about the Algerian tech scene in the next 5 years?
description: 1 = not at all / 5 = very

question: Have you ever started or seriously attempted to build a startup or product?

options.option_text: Yes — I launched something (even if it failed)
I tried but didn't get far
Not yet but I seriously want to
No — it's not something I'm pursuing

question: What was the main reason your startup or product didn't take off?
description: If applicable.

options.option_text: Couldn't find early users or customers
Ran out of time alongside a full-time job
Market in Algeria was too small

question: What's the single biggest obstacle to building a tech startup in Algeria?
description: Pick the one that's most blocking.

options.option_text: No strong startup culture or role models

description: Your actual day-to-day coding style.

options.option_text: AI-assisted — I review and edit AI output
Pair programming with AI most of the time
Mostly prompting and steering AI agents

description: Just your honest opinion.
